Host.title: Host
Host.description: Represents a host in the ChanloPay system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Host entity.

registrationDate.description: Date and time when the host registered.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event for which payments are tracked.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

eventDate.description: Date and time of the event.

qrCode.description: QR code associated with the event.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the event was created.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction made during an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

transactionDate.description: Date and time of the transaction.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used for the transaction.

receiptQrCode.description: The QR code associated with a particular transaction for shareable receipts.

name.description: Name of the person who made the transaction.

village.description: Village of the person who made the transaction.

email.description: Email of the person who made the transaction.

FraudulentTransaction.title: FraudulentTransaction
FraudulentTransaction.description: Represents a transaction flagged as potentially fraudulent by the AI tool.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FraudulentTransaction entity.

reason.description: Reason why the transaction was flagged as fraudulent.

confidenceScore.description: Confidence score of the AI's fraud detection.

reviewStatus.description: The current review status of the transaction

definition.description: Stores host profiles. Only the host (matching {hostId}) can read/write their document.

params.name: hostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the host, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores events created by a host.  Path-based ownership is enforced, meaning only the host (matching {hostId}) can read/write events under this path.

params.name: hostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the host.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

definition.description: Stores transactions for an event. Path-based ownership ensures only the host (matching {hostId}) can read/write transactions under this path.

params.name: hostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the host.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores fraudulent transaction reports for a given transaction. Path-based ownership is enforced, meaning only the host (matching {hostId}) can read/write fraudulent transactions under this path.

params.name: hostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the host.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

params.name: fraudulentTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the fraudulent transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support ChanloPay's core features, including verified host login, event creation, payment tracking, receipt generation and fraud detection. It prioritizes authorization independence and secure list operations. Hosts are stored in a top-level collection. Events are stored within a host-owned subcollection, and transactions are stored as subcollections of events. Fraudulent Transactions are stored as a subcollection of a Transaction.

**Authorization Independence:**
Authorization is achieved by path-based ownership and requires no `get()` calls in security rules. Specifically:
*   `/hosts/{hostId}`: Only the authenticated user with matching `uid` can read/write their profile.
*   `/hosts/{hostId}/events/{eventId}`: Events are owned by the host. The `hostId` in the path enforces this ownership. No denormalization needed, so path based rules are sufficient.
*   `/hosts/{hostId}/events/{eventId}/transactions/{transactionId}`: Transactions are owned by the event.  The path enforces this ownership.
*    `/hosts/{hostId}/events/{eventId}/transactions/{transactionId}/fraudulentTransactions/{fraudulentTransactionId}`: Fraudulent Transactions are owned by the transaction.  The path enforces this ownership.


**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure ensures secure list operations by:
*   Structural Segregation: Different data types (hosts, events, transactions) are stored in separate collections or subcollections, preventing the need to filter based on authorization within a single collection.
*   Path-Based Ownership: Listing events or transactions is restricted by the path, ensuring that a user can only list events/transactions associated with their host ID.
